run_summary: Screen 1 faces a mature portfolio with 7 open positions (2 at or past the 15-day holding window) and a strong basket of 11 fresh flags across three distinct catalysts (Google Vids, OpenAI GPT-5.6 file-deletion, Apple Siri iOS 27). The portfolio is showing mixed results; EVH has hit the window, ONTO is deeply underwater but thesis-intact, and BLKB/NCNO/PAYC are in early recovery. The fresh flags include 3 high-conviction sympathy fades (AMBA, AEIS, VRRM with threat_assessment='none') but the basket rule applies: 8 flags from the Google Vids + OpenAI catalysts alone warrant exploratory tier capping to prevent concentration.

entries.name: Ambarella, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Google Vids now lets you star in your own AI videos with Gemini Omni-powered generation and editing
entries.thesis: Ambarella sells edge-AI inference SoCs for automotive cameras, security cameras, and robotics. Google Vids running personalized AI avatars in the cloud has zero substitution effect on physical edge inference silicon; if anything, more AI everywhere expands Ambarella's TAM. The drop is pure semis-tape sympathy on a big-tech AI headline.
entries.what_kills: Evidence that a major customer (WT/Insta360) is cutting orders, or a broader semis downcycle materializing — but neither is Vids-related.
entries.reasoning: Confidence = 4, threat_assessment = 'none', panic_calibration = 'unjustified'. Filings explicitly position AMBA as edge-AI beneficiary (VLMs, VLAs, reasoning models); Google Vids is cloud-based video generation with zero substitution effect on edge inference silicon. This is a classic sympathy fade on a semis-tape headline unrelated to the business. However, AMBA is part of a basket: Google Vids triggered 3 high-conviction names (AMBA, AEIS, ONTO already held). Per basket rule, downgrade to exploratory tier (6% position size) to avoid 60% concentration on a single trigger read. AMBA alone merits conviction (threat='none', filings clear), but basket rule applies.

red_team.weakest_link: The basket-rule downgrade to exploratory tier assumes the 3-name sympathy basket is mechanically similar, but AMBA's -19.25% five-day move suggests either deeper fundamentals or crowding that pure sympathy alone doesn't explain.
red_team.critique: AMBA's five-day drop of -19.25% paired with volume_multiple of only 1.03 is a specific red flag—the move appears to have momentum/conviction behind it rather than being a thin algo-sympathy fade. The critique correctly identifies zero fundamental threat from Google Vids, but a -19.25% five-day move on 'pure sympathy' is inconsistent with the characterization of the sell as unjustified panic. Either (a) there is other negative news not captured in the flag, or (b) the market is pricing a deeper semis downcycle that the bull case dismisses. The thesis survives on merit (Vids→edge AI is a category non-sequitur), but the magnitude of the move warrants skepticism about whether this is truly isolated to a single headline.

entries.name: Advanced Energy Industries, Inc
entries.catalyst: Google Vids now lets you star in your own AI videos with Gemini Omni-powered generation and editing
entries.thesis: AEIS makes power-conversion equipment for semi fabs and AI data centers. The Google Vids/AI Mode release actually validates the AI infrastructure capex thesis that AEIS is levered to. The move is semi-cap and data-center-adjacent tape sympathy on an AI headline with no fundamental read-through to power delivery hardware.
entries.what_kills: A hyperscaler capex reset or explicit AEIS customer pushout — neither related to Vids.
entries.reasoning: Auto-converted: exploratory tier cap reached (6 simultaneous open). Original reasoning: Confidence = 4, threat_assessment = 'none', panic_calibration = 'unjustified'. Filings identify AI infrastructure capex as a direct demand driver; power-conversion equipment for data centers and fabs has zero exposure to consumer video generation. Google Vids validates the AI capex thesis AEIS is levered to. Classic semis-adjacent tape sympathy. Part of the Google Vids basket (3+ BUY-eligible names from same trigger), so exploratory tier per basket rule, not conviction.

red_team.weakest_link: Volume multiple of 0.67 combined with a -7.56% five-day move suggests retail exit rather than institutionally-driven sympathy selling, indicating possible conviction on a genuine concern the bull case hasn't addressed.
red_team.critique: AEIS presents an unusual pattern: the five-day decline of -7.56% is coupled with below-average volume (0.67x), which contradicts the 'algo sympathy' diagnosis. Below-normal volume on a down move typically signals conviction selling (informed exits) rather than reflex panic. This suggests either (a) informed investors believe power-conversion capex is softening independent of Google Vids, or (b) there is AEIS-specific bad news not reflected in the flag. The bull thesis (power equipment has zero exposure to consumer video) is sound in isolation, but the volume profile suggests the market may be pricing a broader capex slowdown that the thesis glosses over.

entries.name: Tenable Holdings, Inc.
entries.catalyst: OpenAI's new flagship model GPT-5.6 Sol deletes files on its own
entries.thesis: Tenable is a continuous-scan vulnerability management platform with an agent footprint across hybrid infrastructure — the data-collection layer AI can't replicate. AI-generated exploits are described in Tenable's own Risk Factors as accelerating demand for their solutions, not eroding it. A mid-single-digit drop on an OpenAI file-deletion headline is pure thematic selling on a name whose filings say AI is a tailwind.
entries.what_kills: A hyperscaler or CrowdStrike-style rival announcing native vuln-management as a free bundled feature; material contract loss from a Fed customer citing AI supply-chain risk (filings flag Anthropic supply-chain risk designation as a live concern).
entries.reasoning: Auto-converted: exploratory tier cap reached (6 simultaneous open). Original reasoning: Confidence = 4, threat_assessment = 'minimal', panic_calibration = 'unjustified'. Filings explicitly frame AI as a demand tailwind, and Tenable is deploying AI into own products (ExposureAI). AI-generated exploits accelerate demand for vuln scanning, not erode it. However, threat_assessment = 'minimal' (not 'none') means some non-zero exposure, and this is a distinct trigger (OpenAI GPT-5.6 file-deletion) from the Google Vids basket. Exploratory tier (6%) fits the 'minimal threat + unjustified panic' profile without overstating confidence.

red_team.weakest_link: The five-day move of +1.67% contradicts the setup of a sympathy fade, and with threat_assessment=minimal (not 'none'), the critique may be overstating the unjustified-panic case when the stock has already recovered.
red_team.critique: TENB's five-day change of +1.67% directly contradicts the framing of it as an AI-event sympathy fade—the stock is already up over five days, meaning today's -5.77% one-day move appears to be a correction in an already-rebounded name, not sustained panic. If the thesis is sound (AI accelerates vuln demand), the recovery is consistent with that thesis being repriced positively. The only genuine bear case would be if the rebound is itself a false hope and the market is correctly sensing that AI-native security tools (built into models themselves) eventually cannibalize demand for bolt-on scanning. The Tenable filings note is helpful, but the magnitude and direction of the five-day move does not support the 'unjustified panic' framing.

entries.name: Stride, Inc.
entries.catalyst: OpenAI's new flagship model GPT-5.6 Sol deletes files on its own
entries.thesis: Stride operates 89 accredited public charter schools across 31 states. The product is a state-funded, accredited diploma-issuing school — an AI tutor cannot substitute. Retail selling edtech on every OpenAI headline is the textbook sympathy fade the screen exists to catch.
entries.what_kills: State legislative action targeting virtual charters (a real recurring risk in filings, e.g., 2025 Arkansas law); California CDE-style audit reopening; a governor-level policy shift on per-pupil funding for virtual schools.
entries.reasoning: Auto-converted: exploratory tier cap reached (6 simultaneous open). Original reasoning: Confidence = 4, threat_assessment = 'none', panic_calibration = 'unjustified'. Stride operates 89 state-funded accredited charter schools; AI tutor cannot substitute for state-chartered diploma-issuing school. Filings are entirely focused on funding formulas, state charter law, enrollment — no substantive AI threat language. This is a textbook sympathy fade. Part of the OpenAI GPT-5.6 basket (triggered alongside DOCN, DUOL, TENB, and others). Exploratory tier per basket rule (multiple high-confidence 'none' threat names from same trigger).

red_team.weakest_link: The volume multiple of 1.34 on a -3.95% one-day move and -7.16% five-day decline suggests retail conviction selling rather than thin algo sympathy, indicating potential real concerns about enrollment or funding that the 'textbook sympathy fade' framing may underweight.
red_team.critique: LRN's volume profile is notably heavy (1.34x multiple) combined with a -7.16% five-day move, which is inconsistent with the characterization of a light sympathy fade. Elevated volume on a down move signals informed selling pressure, not algorithmic panic. The bull case (state charter schools cannot be substituted by AI tutors) is structurally sound, but the volume pattern suggests institutional investors may be pricing a real threat that the flag dismisses—either enrollment pressure from AI-native alternatives (even if not direct substitutes) or state-level funding concerns independent of the GPT-5.6 headline. The regulatory moat is real, but elevated conviction selling on this move deserves more weight.

entries.name: Tyler Technologies, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Apple opens its new Siri AI to everyone with the iOS 27 public beta
entries.thesis: A consumer voice assistant has no bearing on court records, municipal ERP, or state government workflow software. Tyler's moat is decades of embedded government contracts, procurement cycles measured in years, and jurisdiction-specific compliance. The move is pure thematic vertical-SaaS panic on an Apple headline.
entries.what_kills: Evidence that state/municipal CIOs are actually piloting LLM-native replacements for court records or a large customer defecting to a startup
entries.reasoning: Auto-converted: exploratory tier cap reached (6 simultaneous open). Original reasoning: Confidence = 4, threat_assessment = 'minimal', panic_calibration = 'unjustified'. Tyler's moat is decades of embedded government contracts, multi-year procurement cycles, and jurisdiction-specific compliance. Siri voice assistant has zero bearing on court records or municipal ERP. Filings flag 'political resistance to contracting with third parties' as the real risk, not AI. Threat_assessment = 'minimal' (some non-zero risk from potential AI-native gov solutions, but low probability) justifies exploratory tier. Part of Apple Siri basket.

red_team.weakest_link: Volume multiple of 0.58 on a -6.26% five-day move suggests informed exit rather than sympathy selling, raising the possibility that institutional investors are pricing a genuine threat to Tyler's government procurement moat that the bull case underestimated.
red_team.critique: TYL's below-average volume (0.58x) combined with a -6.26% five-day decline is the opposite of a sympathy-fade signature. Low volume on sustained down move suggests informed, conviction-based selling by holders who believe something material has changed. The bull thesis (government procurement moats + decades of embeddedness) is sound in isolation, but the volume pattern indicates professional investors may be pricing a scenario the flag dismisses—either (a) AI-native competitors actually winning government RFPs faster than traditional analysis suggests, or (b) Tyler's own ability to upgrade customers on AI-powered features is being questioned. The procurement cycle is indeed slow, but that makes the conviction selling more notable, not less.

entries.name: Verra Mobility Corporation
entries.catalyst: Apple opens its new Siri AI to everyone with the iOS 27 public beta
entries.thesis: Siri cannot photograph a speeding car, generate a court-admissible violation, or replace toll transponders. VRRM's business is physical infrastructure plus government contracts plus legal evidentiary process. The sympathy tag is mechanically wrong.
entries.what_kills: Loss of a top-3 commercial services customer during pending renewals (flagged in Q1 10-Q); adverse ballot initiative in a major state
entries.reasoning: Auto-converted: exploratory tier cap reached (6 simultaneous open). Original reasoning: Confidence = 4, threat_assessment = 'none', panic_calibration = 'unjustified'. VRRM's business is physical speeding/toll cameras, government contracts (new NYCDOT 5-year contract Jan 2026), and court-admissible evidentiary process. Siri cannot photograph cars or generate legal violations. Filings' AI risk factor discusses VRRM's own AI adoption and self-driving reducing long-term violations, not displacement. Classic government-infrastructure sympathy fade on consumer AI headline. Part of Apple Siri basket (multiple BUY-eligible names from same trigger). Exploratory tier per basket rule.

red_team.weakest_link: The five-day move of +2.84% directly contradicts the sympathy-fade diagnosis, and a volume multiple of 0.46 suggests the stock has already been discounted by informed sellers; the setup assumes panic that the price action does not support.
red_team.critique: VRRM's five-day change is positive (+2.84%) despite the flag's classification as a sympathy-fade victim, and volume is well below average (0.46x). This price-and-volume pattern indicates either (a) the stock was already repriced downward in prior periods and today is a rebound, or (b) the market never fully believed the sympathy thesis in the first place. The bull case (Siri cannot photograph cars) is sound, but the positive five-day drift and low volume together suggest the market is efficiently pricing the irrelevance of Siri to toll infrastructure—not panicking. The flag's framing as a 'panic fade' is not supported by the price action.

entries.name: Evolent Health, Inc
entries.catalyst: Google Vids now lets you star in your own AI videos with Gemini Omni-powered generation and editing
entries.thesis: Evolent's product is capitated risk arrangements with payers for specialty care (oncology, cardiology). A Google consumer video product cannot substitute for provider contracts, clinical networks, or risk-bearing licenses. Move looks like broad healthcare-SaaS sympathy on an AI headline unrelated to value-based care.
entries.what_kills: Evolent already faces genuine business headwinds (oncology cost pressure, customer concentration) that could dwarf sympathy noise — a real earnings miss would not be an unjustified panic.
entries.reasoning: Flag shows confidence = 3, threat_assessment = 'none', panic_calibration = 'unjustified' — but EVH is already in portfolio at days_held = 15 (at window max). Filings note Evolent faces genuine business headwinds (oncology cost, customer concentration) that the flag acknowledges. Thesis is intact but window is exhausted. Do not re-buy; the position is already scheduled for exit. This flag is informational validation of the existing position's exit decision, not a new entry signal.

entries.name: DigitalOcean Holdings, Inc.
entries.catalyst: OpenAI's new flagship model GPT-5.6 Sol deletes files on its own
entries.thesis: DOCN's SMB/indie-developer niche is structurally underserved by AWS/Azure/GCP; they are packaging AI inference for that segment (Paperspace acquisition, GPU droplets). An OpenAI model safety headline doesn't touch DOCN's economics. That said, the five-day drop is deep enough that some real fundamental concern (perhaps an earnings preannouncement whisper) could be running through the tape — hence partial calibration.
entries.what_kills: A pre-announcement of soft SMB revenue; hyperscaler cutting inference API prices below DOCN's sustainable levels; customer churn spike in AI/ML segment.
entries.reasoning: Already in portfolio at days_held = 2, currently scheduled for TRIM. Flag shows confidence = 3 (at min buy threshold), threat_assessment = 'indirect', panic_calibration = 'partial' — weaker profile than pure sympathy fades. Filings acknowledge real hyperscaler competition, AI/ML customer switching risk, and SMB segment exposure. The five-day drop of -14.88% is deep and may reflect genuine concern beyond the headline. Position decision (TRIM) is appropriate; do not layer into a position already flagged as structurally exposed.

entries.name: Duolingo, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Apple opens its new Siri AI to everyone with the iOS 27 public beta
entries.thesis: Unlike accredited K-12, consumer language learning IS meaningfully AI-exposed — DUOL's own filings admit it. But the moat is the gamified habit loop (streaks, leagues, social pressure), not raw instructional quality, and Siri is a voice assistant, not a language tutor. The relatively modest move suggests the market is pricing this correctly-ish; classify as partial overshoot rather than pure sympathy fade. Confidence lowered because unlike the vertical-SaaS names, there IS a real long-term AI question here.
entries.what_kills: DAU declines, drop in paid subscriber conversion, or Apple/Google integrating a free language tutor directly into their OS
entries.reasoning: Already in portfolio at days_held = 2, scheduled for TRIM. Flag confidence = 3, threat_assessment = 'indirect', panic_calibration = 'partial'. Unlike accredited K-12 or government SaaS, consumer language learning faces genuine AI-native competitive threats (free ChatGPT, translation APIs); filings explicitly name this. This is NOT a pure sympathy fade — DUOL's moat (gamified habit loop) is real, but the AI question is substantive. Position decision (TRIM) reflects the mixed thesis; do not add.

entries.name: Blackbaud, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Apple opens its new Siri AI to everyone with the iOS 27 public beta
entries.thesis: Siri doesn't manage donor CRM, 501(c)(3) accounting, or process fundraising payments. Blackbaud's nonprofit customers have 20+ years of donor history embedded; switching costs compound with each renewal cycle. The move is pure vertical-SaaS sympathy on a consumer AI headline.
entries.what_kills: A large customer (major university/healthcare system) publicly migrating to an AI-native donor management platform; deceleration in subscription bookings
entries.reasoning: Already in portfolio at days_held = 6, currently HOLD. Flag shows confidence = 4, threat_assessment = 'minimal', panic_calibration = 'unjustified' — solid sympathy-fade read. However, position is already flagged at +19.85% and thesis is intact. Do not ADD (Screen 1 does not support ADD). The flag validates the existing position hold; no new action warranted.
